name: AdV2_ITR_inverted_terminal_repeat
description: AdV2 inverted terminal repeats (ITRs): ~103 bp identical sequences at both ends of the linear dsDNA genome in head-to-head orientation. Each ITR carries the origin of replication (within nt 1-51 of each end), the core origin (nt 1-18) bound by pTP and AdPol, and auxiliary regions binding NFI/CTF (nt ~19-40) and NFIII/Oct-1 (nt ~39-51). Species C ITRs (Ad2/5) are ~103 bp; species F ITRs (Ad40/41) are longer (~163/195 bp). Protein-primed replication initiates with pTP-dCTP covalent intermediate templated by the second G of the 3' GGGCG end. ITR sequence identity creates apparent dual mapping for any short read falling near either end — require_psv_disambig + itr_aware essential.
expected_copy_count: 2 identical ITRs per genome (left and right ends)
